name: mcp-builder
tagline: Phased guide (research, implement, review/test, evaluate) for building MCP servers in TypeScript or Python.
verdict: Well-structured, security-clean dev guide with a clear four-phase workflow, concrete commands, and good context economy via reference files. Weak spots: no negative-trigger scoping, no failure handling for build/test steps, no re-run/idempotency guidance for server scaffolding, no version/changelog signal, and MCP Inspector CLI use isn't declared upfront.

highlights.summary: No 'NOT for' / exclusion / out-of-scope language anywhere in description or body (line 3 description has no exclusions).
Build/test steps like 'Run `npm run build` to verify compilation' (line 140) have no stated fallback or stop condition if the build/tests fail.
Multi-step server creation ('Create shared utilities:' line 88, tool implementation) has no guidance on re-running after a partial/prior build.
